title: Generate a D-efficient CBC design
summary: Construct a multi-version CBC design that maximises log|M| of the MNL information matrix under effects coding. Supports point or Bayesian (multi-draw) priors on respondent utilities.
description: Encodes each profile under L-1 effects coding (the last level becomes -1 across all parameters) and accumulates the per-task information matrix G = Σ_c q_c (z_c - μ)(z_c - μ)^T, where q_c is the choice probability under softmax(z_c · β) (or 1/C when β is omitted). The objective maximises log|M_total| + α · mean_v log|M_v|, with M_total = Σ_v Σ_t G_{v,t}/V (so it is on the per-version scale) and M_v = Σ_t G_{v,t}. Bayesian priors are supported: pass `weights.beta` as either a single vector of length p (effects-coded parameter count) or an array of draws, in which case the objective averages across draws.
usage:
[["design-cbc::design-de"]]
run_dir = "<path>"
run_name = "<id>"
# spec.* = the CBC structural inputs
# optional: weights.alpha, weights.ridge, weights.beta (Single | Draws | null)

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Workspace directory; primary output goes to {run_dir}/{run_name}_design.csv.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Run identifier; used as a filename prefix and to namespace ocinfo files.

arguments.name: spec
arguments.description: CBC spec input. Required: versions, tasks_per_version, concepts_per_task, attributes (each with name + max_level). Optional: pairwise_restrictions, gating_rules, fixed_cells, fixed_attributes, level_weights.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Objective weighting and prior inputs. Keys: alpha, ridge, beta.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Weight on the per-version mean log|M_v| relative to log|M_total|.

parameters.name: ridge
parameters.description: Diagonal ridge added to information matrices to keep them positive definite.

parameters.name: beta
parameters.description: Optional MNL coefficients. Either a single vector of length p (effects-coded params) for a point prior, or an array of vectors for a Bayesian average across draws. Omit (or null) to use uniform 1/C choice probabilities.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Base seed; per-start seeds derived as base + i.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Worker threads for parallel restarts (only meaningful when n_starts > 1).

value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:
- `{run_name}_design.csv` — wide design (Version, Task, Concept, <attribute columns>).
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/design_long.csv` — long design (one row per cell).
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/summary_overview.csv` — final score, iterations, accepted, n_parameters, log|M_total|, per-version log|M_v|.
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/summary_main_counts.csv` — per-attribute level counts.
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/design.json`, `summary.json`, `spec.input.json` — machine-readable outputs.

The summary's `n_parameters` equals the total effects-coded parameter count (Σ_k max_level_k). For multi-draw beta the reported logdets are averaged across draws.
notes: Length of each `beta` draw must equal the number of effects-coded parameters p = Σ_k max_level_k. The parameter ordering follows the attribute order in `spec.attributes`, with each block of size max_level_k for attribute k.
The `ridge` parameter is essential when using strong restrictions or fixed cells — without it the information matrix can become singular for degenerate proposals.
Score is reported as `-objective`, so lower is better in the SA loop and in `summary.final_score`.
Multi-start (`n_starts` 4–8 with matched `threads`) is recommended because the D-efficient surface is multimodal under restrictions.

see_also.description: Balance / minimum-overlap variant; faster and prior-free.
